page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-8 is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8581C processor. It features 8 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The processor is equipped with 48 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 260 MB of L3 cache. Memory capacity is 30 GB, providing 3.75 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and has zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-threaded performance. The instance achieves top-tier status in Geekbench single-core testing with a score of 2203.0, Geekbench text processing at 3031.0, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression at 14.84 GB/sec. Single-threaded gzip compression is also strong, placing in the top 25 percent at 34.68 GB/sec. Multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks generally fall into the average tier, with a PassMark CPU score of 12708.61 and a multi-core Geekbench score of 8381.0. Memory read-only bandwidth ranges from 668.72 GB/sec for cached small blocks to 61.17 GB/sec for uncached large blocks.

While the instance demonstrates capable processing power for general workloads, it exhibits limitations in demanding machine learning tasks. LLM text generation using a medium 7B model is weak, yielding only 7.18 tokens per second, and there is no capability for larger 70B models due to the lack of hardware accelerators. However, its strong single-core capabilities and balanced memory-to-CPU ratio make it suitable for web serving, software compilation, database operations, and text processing workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-8 is an x86_64 general-purpose server instance configured with 8 vCPUs and 30 GB of RAM. Powered by a dedicated 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon 8581C processor with 4 physical cores and a 260 MB L3 cache, this instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show top-tier single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2203.0 and strong single-threaded decompression speeds. Multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall within the average range. Due to the absence of a GPU, LLM text generation performance is weak on medium models and unsupported on large models. This instance is best suited for workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, text processing, and general-purpose database operations where high single-threaded CPU performance is required but local high-speed storage is not a necessity.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-8 is an x86_64 server with 8 vCPUs, 30 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C CPU, offering top-tier single-core performance for text processing and web serving.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-8 features 8 vCPUs, 30 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, delivering strong single-core performance for general workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing and strong single-core performance for balanced general-purpose compute workloads.
bullet_points: 8 vCPUs and 30 GB RAM powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8581C processor
Top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and text processing benchmarks
No local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators included
Average multi-core performance and memory bandwidth
Suited for web serving, software compilation, and database operations
